# Thread Dump Doctor

> A free, privacy-first Java thread dump analyzer that runs 100% in your browser. No data is ever uploaded or sent to any server.

## What it does

Thread Dump Doctor analyzes HotSpot JVM thread dumps (produced by `jstack` or `kill -3`) and instantly diagnoses common concurrency problems in Java applications.

## URL

https://kushagrasri.github.io/thread-dump-doctor/

## Problems it detects

- **Deadlocks** — JVM-reported deadlocks and classic A→B→A lock-cycle deadlocks
- **Pool self-starvation** — threads inside a pool submitting tasks back to the same pool and blocking on `.get()` or `.join()` (Triangle B / CompletableFuture pattern)
- **Socket-read starvation** — pool threads blocked on blocking socket reads (Triangle C)
- **Callback-thread hijacking** — user callbacks running on upstream I/O threads (OkHttp, Netty)
- **In-callback blocking** — `.join()` or `.get()` calls inside CompletableFuture callbacks
- **Thread pool saturation** — all workers busy, no idle threads remaining
- **ForkJoinPool.commonPool() overload** — shared pool heavily occupied, starving unrelated workloads
- **Unbounded WAITING** — threads waiting with no timeout in user/application code
- **Four-bucket triage** — classifies every thread into: noise, framework-idle, app-working, or app-stuck

## Diff mode

Upload two thread dumps taken ~30 seconds apart to detect threads that have not made progress — a reliable way to confirm a live stall or deadlock.

## How to use

1. Visit https://kushagrasri.github.io/thread-dump-doctor/
2. Drag and drop a `.txt` file from `jstack <pid>` or `kill -3 <pid>`
3. Click **Analyze**
4. For diff mode, upload two files and click **Run two-dump diff**

## Input formats supported

- OpenJDK / Oracle JDK `jstack` output
- `kill -3` signal output (HotSpot JVM)
- VisualVM thread dump export
- GraalVM (partial support)

## Privacy

All processing runs locally in JavaScript. No network requests are made with your thread dump data. The tool works fully offline after the page loads.

## Technology

Single standalone HTML file. No backend, no framework, no dependencies beyond Google Fonts. The entire analyzer is ~1000 lines of vanilla JavaScript.

## Source

https://github.com/kushagrasri/thread-dump-doctor
